title: Work-Notebooks — light notebook authoring mode
summary: Add a light 'work-notebook' mode alongside the BERIL kbu path: an idempotent kbu notebook-init scaffolder, an in-repo kbu-run authoring loop, and a kbu-workbench maintainer console. Drops manifest/state-machine/subprojects; keeps and parametrizes the NotebookSession cache layer; rewrites jupyter-dev as the work-notebook structure skill; forks a manifest-free synthesize. Two disjoint skill-deployment surfaces (work-notebook vs BERIL).
key_decisions: Parallel surface to BERIL; BERIL path left untouched (no migration).
Two disjoint skill-deployment surfaces; no shared skill files.
Notebook naming: bare NN_snake_case.ipynb (BERIL-match); PRJ-<topic>/ folders; NBCache/NBOutput; models/genomes/data under notebooks/.
Keep + parametrize NotebookSession cache dir (NBCache for work, .kbcache default for BERIL).
kbu-run hands off to JupyterLab for running; debugs on report; saves AIAssistant session records.
notebook-init registers repo in AIAssistant registry + writes notebooks/.kbu-run.json project binding.
jupyter-dev rewritten as authoritative work-notebook structure skill.
synthesize forked manifest-free -> REPORT.md per PRJ.
New distinct home command kbu-workbench; kbu-start left to BERIL.
Bundle update folded into kbu notebook-init --update.
Bundle deployment gated on ClaudeCommands presence (claude-skills work-notebook collection); graceful degradation otherwise.
Modeling-skill fork (BERIL-free kbu-fba) deferred to fast-follow PRD.
out_of_scope: Forking kbu-fba into a BERIL-free modeling skill (deferred).
Removing BERIL manifest/state-machine/subagent code from the codebase.
